subject: Biology
title: How Plants Drink: Water Transport
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Plants Drink: Water Transport.

hook.text: A tall tree moves hundreds of liters of water from its roots to its leaves every day—with no pump.

buildup.text: Water enters through root hairs by osmosis and travels up through xylem tubes.

discovery.text: Transpiration—water evaporating from leaf pores—creates a pulling force that draws water upward.

twist.text: Water molecules stick to each other through cohesion, forming an unbroken chain from root to leaf.

climax.text: This system lifts water over 100 meters in the tallest trees—defying gravity without muscles.

punchline.text: No heart, no pump. Just physics and biology.

quiz.question: What force pulls water up through a tree?
